summary: The unit test suite in matrix-generator.test.ts covers all major stated requirements: cartesian product generation, include/exclude rules, max-parallel, fail-fast defaults and explicit values, and size validation including the subtle 'matching includes don't count as new jobs' distinction. The act-runner.test.ts adds good workflow structure tests (trigger presence, file references, actionlint) and per-fixture integration tests with exact value assertions. Design is clean: clear TDD RED/GREEN comments, independent tests, a well-factored helper layer (copyDir, initGitRepo, runActWithFixture, extractMatrixJson), and an afterAll flush for the required artifact. Gaps that cap the score: (1) numeric dimension values (the interface allows `number[]`) are never tested; (2) invalid/malformed input (null values, missing required keys, negative maxParallel) is untested — the generator would silently misbehave rather than throw; (3) exclude entries that match zero base combinations are not exercised; (4) the CLI entry-point error path (missing config file, unreadable file, size-exceeded exit-code 1) has no coverage; (5) 'fail-fast defaults to false' is repeated across three separate describe blocks with no additional behaviour exercised each time; (6) the extractMatrixJson regex approach to strip act prefixes is fragile — a slight change in act's output format would silently produce empty JSON strings and make assertions vacuous rather than failing loudly.
